serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving consists of approximately 200 g grilled salmon fillet accompanied by approximately 150 g mixed garden salad, totalling around 350 g per plate.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, cooked, dry heat (USDA FoodData Central ID 175168) combined with Mixed salad greens, raw (USDA FoodData Central ID 169248); composite estimated at approximately 57% salmon and 43% salad by weight
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for grilled salmon with garden salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central using a composite of grilled Atlantic salmon (57% by weight, ~200 g of 350 g serving) and mixed salad greens (43% by weight, ~150 g of 350 g serving). Per-100g values are weighted accordingly. Salmon values: energy 206 kcal, protein 22.1 g, fat 12.4 g, sat fat 2.7 g, carb 0 g, fibre 0 g, sodium 59 mg per 100 g. Salad greens values: energy 17 kcal, protein 1.6 g, fat 0.3 g, sat fat 0.04 g, carb 2.9 g, fibre 1.8 g, sodium 28 mg per 100 g. Composite per 100 g: energy ~124 kcal, protein ~13.6 g, fat ~7.2 g, sat fat ~1.6 g, carb ~1.2 g, fibre ~0.8 g, sodium ~46 mg.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; grilled fish and non-starchy vegetable salad composite analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grilled salmon with garden salad. Salmon contains negligible carbohydrate and has an effectively zero glycemic response; non-starchy salad greens similarly have a very low GI. A composite GI of 25 is estimated by analogy with grilled fish (GI ~0 due to near-zero carbohydrate) and non-starchy vegetables (GI ~15–32 per University of Sydney GI Database). The composite GI of 25 reflects the low-carbohydrate nature of the dish. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (25 × 4.2) / 100 ≈ 1.1, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving ≈ 4.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~46 mg per 100 g) and high potassium (~380 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Grilled salmon with garden salad is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its low sodium content and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio, which supports blood pressure regulation; however, patients should confirm that no high-sodium dressings or sauces are added at the restaurant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (~1.2 g per 100 g) and low glycemic index (~25)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled salmon with garden salad is an excellent choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as its very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index result in minimal postprandial glucose excursion; the high protein and omega-3 fatty acid content further support insulin sensitivity and metabolic health.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (EPA and DHA) from Atlantic salmon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled salmon is a rich source of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which are clinically associated with reduced triglycerides, anti-inflammatory effects, and reduced cardiovascular event risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease are encouraged to consume oily fish such as salmon at least twice weekly per AHA guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Salmon with Garden Salad is a nutritionally balanced restaurant dish consisting of a grilled Atlantic salmon fillet served alongside a fresh mixed garden salad of lettuce, cucumber, tomato, and leafy greens, typically dressed with a light olive oil vinaigrette. The salmon is cooked over direct dry heat (grilling), which preserves its natural omega-3 fatty acid content while producing a lightly charred, flavourful exterior and a moist, flaky interior. The dish is high in protein (~13.6 g per 100 g), rich in long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (EPA and DHA), and low in carbohydrates (~1.2 g per 100 g), making it one of the most clinically recommended meal options for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension. Its low glycemic index (~25) and low glycemic load per serving (~1.1) mean it produces minimal postprandial blood glucose elevation. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. It is served at restaurants in Singapore including Western-style and international cuisine establishments. Nutritionally, it provides significant vitamin D (~8.2 mcg per 100 g), vitamin B12 (~2.4 mcg per 100 g), selenium (~28 mcg per 100 g), and potassium (~380 mg per 100 g). The garden salad component contributes dietary fibre, vitamin C, vitamin A, and antioxidant phytonutrients. This dish is suitable for weight management, heart health, and blood sugar control diets.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for grilled salmon with garden salad was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central using a weighted composite of grilled Atlantic salmon and mixed salad greens at approximately 57:43 ratio by weight. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for grilled fish and non-starchy vegetables. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite estimation approach and absence of a direct Singapore-specific source entry.
notes: 1. Cuisine tag in input JSON was listed as 'Indian' and 'Chicken Dish', which are both incorrect for this dish. The food is a Western/International grilled fish dish with no Indian or chicken components. Cuisine has been corrected to 'Western / International'. 2. The input cuisine_tags ('Indian', 'Chicken Dish') appear to be field survey platform tagging errors and have been disregarded. 3. Nutrient values are composite estimates from USDA FoodData Central (grilled Atlantic salmon FDCID 175168 + mixed salad greens FDCID 169248) at 57:43 weight ratio. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for grilled Atlantic salmon and salad greens — confirmed zero. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (~2.8) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (~2.4) are estimated from USDA salmon fatty acid profile. 6. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Halal status is assumed as salmon is a permissible fish under Islamic dietary law, but restaurant-level halal certification has not been verified. 8. The serving size of 350 g (200 g salmon + 150 g salad) is a reasonable restaurant-portion estimate; actual restaurant portion may vary. 9. Sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and all micronutrients are populated from USDA data where available; no fields are set to -1 in this record as USDA provides comprehensive coverage for salmon. 10. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this dish is served in Singapore, even though it is of Western/International origin.
